profile_backing.source: fresh rch Criterion baseline before source edit

lever.name: dense F64 transpose output path
lever.description: For rank-2 permutation [1,0], attempted to emit well-formed F64 transpose output through TensorValue::new_f64_values while preserving the exact tiled source-index mapping and falling back for malformed declared-F64 tensors.
lever.alien_primitive: cache-local typed/SoA layout for vectorized data movement

isomorphism_proof.index_order: candidate wrote out[j * rows + i] = in[i * cols + j], identical to the existing rank-2 tiled literal path
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: transpose performs no arithmetic; F64Bits were reconstructed with f64::from_bits, preserving signed zero and NaN payloads
isomorphism_proof.errors: arity, permutation parsing/validation, stride overflow checks, empty tensors, non-F64 tensors, and malformed declared-F64 tensors kept existing paths

validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax transpose_rank2 -- --nocapture
validation.details: 4 tests passed in the candidate state: dense input bit preservation, literal input bit preservation, malformed fallback, and non-block-aligned rank-2 transpose

rebench.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline eval/transpose_256x256_f64
rebench.outliers: 7/100; 1 low mild, 1 high mild, 5 high severe

subagent_review.finding: Value semantics are preserved only with strict gating, but LiteralBuffer::as_f64_slice is public, so changing output storage is representation-observable. Pack from as_f64_slice first for dense inputs; avoid Literal::as_f64 because it coerces non-F64 literals.

next_primitive.reason: The output-wrapper attempt doubled latency; the next pass should avoid literal materialization before transpose by keeping the producing operation and transpose in typed dense storage, rather than wrapping a literal-backed output after the fact.
next_primitive.target_ratio: >=2.0x on a dense-input transpose profile row
